fields.caption: your AI model can't fail government vetting / if changing the system prompt makes it a new model every time

narrative_summary: The joke exploits the story's own absurdity: since nobody can define when a system prompt change constitutes a 'new model,' you could theoretically dodge re-review forever by just tweaking the prompt — smug self-defeating logic applied to a real unanswered policy question.
rationale: Candidates 1 and 5 tie on raw score but Candidate 1's phrasing is tighter — 'every time' at the end adds a slight escalating rhythm that makes the self-defeating logic land harder than Candidate 5's more neutral 'brand new model.' Roll Safe is the ideal template for smug circular logic, and the system-prompt loophole is the sharpest specific detail the story offers.
story_title: White House Considers Vetting A.I. Models Before They Are Released
place: at a gas station in the desert
